english: standard; fifth of the ten heavenly stems

etymology.description: Depicts a flail and a threshing device
etymology.interpretation: The character 庚 (gēng, "standard; fifth of the ten heavenly stems") originally depicted a flail and a threshing device used in agriculture, illustrating its role connected to the cycles and standards in agricultural work, which were fundamental in traditional Chinese calendars and reckoning systems.

examples.chinese: 庚是天干的第五位(庚是天干的第五位)。
examples.pinyin: Gēng shì tiāngān de dì wǔ wèi.
examples.english: Geng is the fifth of the ten heavenly stems.

examples.chinese: 在古代历法中，庚被用作记录年份的符号(在古代曆法中，庚被用作記錄年份的符號)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gǔdài lìfǎ zhōng, gēng bèi yòng zuò jìlù niánfèn de fúhào.
examples.english: In ancient calendars, Geng was used as a symbol to record the year.

examples.chinese: 庚日适合举办婚礼(庚日適合舉辦婚禮)，因为它象征着新开始(因為它象徵著新開始)。
examples.pinyin: Gēng rì shìhé jǔbàn hūnlǐ, yīnwèi tā xiàngzhēngzhe xīn kāishǐ.
examples.english: The Geng day is suitable for holding weddings because it symbolizes a new beginning.
